mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized contraction in household spending, which reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when housing and credit diverge, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding its 12-month moving average signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production rises while equity valuations contract due to margin compression, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently above the long-term trend rate of 2.0 percent confirms economic momentum, which sustains earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth remains elevated while equity returns turn negative due to valuation mean reversion, or the rolling correlation between GDP surprises and SPX returns falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate crossing above 4.5 percent imposes restrictive financial conditions that elevate borrowing costs for equities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite funds rates exceeding 4.5 percent due to AI-driven productivity shocks, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth and unemployment declines occur alongside equity drawdowns due to stagflation fears, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for inflation surprises.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer credit outstanding accelerating above its 6-month moving average indicates leveraged household spending, which temporarily inflates retail and service sector earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Credit growth accelerates while equity returns turn negative due to debt sustainability concerns, or the signal fails to predict returns when credit cycles peak and reverse.
